scenario_summary: An author attempts to add a new public command to the hotfix branch alongside the security fix it carries. The hotfix_train_ref branch posture is stabilisation_no_feature_invention; backport_lane.stable_to_hotfix forbids new_feature_invention, public_api_widening, and claim_widening. The proposed change satisfies the security_fix admissible class but also tries to introduce new_feature_invention, which is in forbidden_change_classes. The lane refuses the change and routes the new feature back to mainline.

explanation_fields.user_facing_summary: Hotfix releases ship one named correction at a time. New features must land on the mainline first, not on a hotfix train.
explanation_fields.release_notes_summary: backport_lane.stable_to_hotfix admits only security fixes and data-loss / trust corrections. New feature invention, public API widening, and claim widening on the hotfix train are non-conforming.
explanation_fields.support_summary: Support packets cite the backport_lane.stable_to_hotfix row and the forbidden_change_classes that triggered the refusal so reviewers can confirm the rejection rule mechanically.
explanation_fields.promotion_summary: Refuse. The lane row pins forbidden_change_classes including new_feature_invention; the matrix cites this rule so the refusal is named, not narrated.

notes: Reference fixture for the forbidden_change_classes guard on backport_lane.stable_to_hotfix.
